  is a mobile layout fix.

‚Źļ Now I understand the problems clearly. Let me catalog what the screenshots
  show:

  1. Header (PageTopbar) ‚ÄĒ logo + "git for your vibes" tagline wrapping
  one-word-per-line + "Sign in with GitHub" + theme toggle, all crammed; causes
  overflow.
  2. Trace card grid (Explore) ‚ÄĒ card titles wrapping one-word-per-line, hash
  codes bleeding behind text, cards extending past the viewport (horizontal
  scroll).
  3. Viewer topbar ‚ÄĒ breadcrumb vibeshub / vibeshub / vibeshub / trace/6e9...
  and the PROMPTS chip row overflow horizontally.
  4. Footer text clipped on the left from the same overflow.

  The git status shows you've already started on exactly these files. Let me see
  the current state before changing anything.

‚Źļ Bash(sed -n '1,40p' package.json && echo "=== playwright cfg ===" && cat

  ‚éŅ ¬†{
       "name": "vibeshub-frontend",

     ‚Ä¶ +50 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ I now have the complete picture. Let me map the root causes before editing:

  Horizontal overflow (screenshots 4, 5): the topbars are display:flex rows
  whose children (Sign in with GitHub button, breadcrumb links) are
  white-space:nowrap and don't shrink, plus 32px side padding. On a phone the
  row is wider than the viewport, and nothing clips it, so the whole page
  scrolls sideways. There's no global overflow-x guard either.

  Crowded Landing header (screenshot 1): the "git for your vibes" tagline is a
  flex item with no nowrap, so it gets squeezed and wraps one word per line.

  Crowded Browse grid (screenshot 3): .traceRow is auto 1fr auto; the right
  column holds the short_id hash with white-space:nowrap, forcing it wide,
  squeezing the title's 1fr to min-content so it wraps one word per line and the
  hash visually collides. Landing.module.css has no mobile rule for this row.

  Viewer topbar (screenshot 5): breadcrumb vibeshub / vibeshub / vibeshub / 
  trace/‚Ä¶ + Share text + theme don't fit; footer's long session UUID is nowrap.

  The plan: (1) add a global overflow-x guard, (2) make all three topbars
  mobile-friendly (smaller padding, shorter auth CTA, icon-only Share, drop the
  redundant owner crumb + tagline), (3) reflow .traceRow on mobile and drop the
  redundant hash. Let me make the edits.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/styles/globals.css)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 3 lines
       8  body {
       9    margin: 0;
      10    padding: 0;
      11 +  /* Guard against any single wide element forcing a sideways s
         +croll on mobile.                                               
      12 +     Set on html only so sticky topbars keep working against th
         +e viewport. */                                                 
      13 +  overflow-x: hidden;                                          
      14    background: var(--bg);
      15    color: var(--text);
      16    font-family: var(--font-sans);
‚Źļ I should target only html for the overflow guard (putting it on body too can
  make body a scroll container and interfere with sticky). Let me correct that.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/styles/globals.css)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 6 lines, removed 3 lines
       4    box-sizing: border-box;
       5  }
       6  
       7 +/* Guard against any single wide element forcing a sideways scr
         +oll on mobile.                                                 
       8 +   Scoped to html (not body) so the sticky topbars keep stickin
         +g to the viewport. */                                          
       9 +html {                                                         
      10 +  overflow-x: hidden;                                          
      11 +}                                                              
      12 +                                                               
      13  html,
      14  body {
      15    margin: 0;
      16    padding: 0;
      11 -  /* Guard against any single wide element forcing a sideways s
         -croll on mobile.                                               
      12 -     Set on html only so sticky topbars keep working against th
         -e viewport. */

      17    background: var(--bg);
      18    color: var(--text);
      19    font-family: var(--font-sans);

     ‚Ä¶ +38 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ The guard prevents the scrollbar, but scrollWidth reveals real >viewport
  elements still being clipped ‚ÄĒ I need to find and fix the actual culprits, not
  just clip them. My offender detection flagged elements inside the
  horizontally-scrollable jumpstrip (false positives). Let me improve the
  diagnostic to ignore elements inside overflow-x:auto/scroll containers and
  sort by width, then re-run.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts)  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 22 lines, removed 11 lines
       84  async function expectNoHScroll(page: import("@playwright/test"
           ).Page) {
       85    const overflow = await page.evaluate(() => {
       86      const de = document.documentElement;
       87 -    return {                                                  
       88 -      scrollW: de.scrollWidth,                                
       89 -      clientW: de.clientWidth,                                
       90 -      // Any element wider than the viewport is a culprit.    
       91 -      widest: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("body *")) 
       92 -        .filter((el) => (el as HTMLElement).getBoundingClientR
          -ect().right > de.clientWidth + 1)                             
       93 -        .slice(0, 5)                                          
       94 -        .map((el) => {                                        
       95 -          const e = el as HTMLElement;                        
       96 -          return `${e.tagName.toLowerCase()}.${(e.className ||
          - "").toString().slice(0, 40)} -> ${Math.round(e.getBoundingCli
          -entRect().right)}`;                                           
       97 -        }),                                                   
       87 +    const cw = de.clientWidth;                                
       88 +    // An element only forces page-level overflow if it (and n
          +one of its                                                    
       89 +    // ancestors) lives inside a horizontally scrollable/clipp
          +ing box.                                                      
       90 +    const inScroller = (el: Element) => {                     
       91 +      let p = el.parentElement;                               
       92 +      while (p && p !== document.body) {                      
       93 +        const ox = getComputedStyle(p).overflowX;             
       94 +        if (ox === "auto" || ox === "scroll" || ox === "hidden
          +" || ox === "clip")                                           
       95 +          return true;                                        
       96 +        p = p.parentElement;                                  
       97 +      }                                                       
       98 +      return false;                                           
       99      };
      100 +    const widest = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("body 
          +*"))                                                          
      101 +      .map((el) => ({ el: el as HTMLElement, r: (el as HTMLEle
          +ment).getBoundingClientRect() }))                             
      102 +      .filter((x) => x.r.right > cw + 1 && !inScroller(x.el)) 
      103 +      .sort((a, b) => b.r.right - a.r.right)                  
      104 +      .slice(0, 6)                                            
      105 +      .map(                                                   
      106 +        (x) =>                                                
      107 +          `${x.el.tagName.toLowerCase()}.${(x.el.className || 
          +"").toString().slice(0, 36)} -> ${Math.round(x.r.right)}`,    
      108 +      );                                                      
      109 +    return { scrollW: de.scrollWidth, clientW: cw, widest };  
      110    });
      111    expect(
      112      overflow.scrollW,

‚Źļ Bash(npx playwright test --config=e2e/playwright.config.ts mobile-verify 2>&1
      | grep -E "offenders|passed|failed|Received|Expected|‚úď|‚úė|‚Äļ" | head -40;
      echo "exit: $?")
  ‚éŅ ¬†  ‚úė  1 e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts:117:1 ‚Äļ landing has no horizontal ove
     rflow on mobile (2.0s)
       ‚úď  2 e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts:124:1 ‚Äļ repo page has no horizontal o
     ‚Ä¶ +17 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 3m)

‚Źļ Real offenders now. Trace viewer: the whole .thread/.viewer-body chain is
  pinned to exactly 760px (--content-width), which means a wide unbreakable
  descendant is forcing the flex chain to its max-width and min-width:auto is
  propagating it up. Landing: a 423px element (32px over). Let me find the leaf
  culprits and inspect the fixture's widest lines.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 13 lines, removed 5 lines
      100      const widest = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("body 
           *"))
      101        .map((el) => ({ el: el as HTMLElement, r: (el as HTMLEle
           ment).getBoundingClientRect() }))
      102        .filter((x) => x.r.right > cw + 1 && !inScroller(x.el))
      103 -      .sort((a, b) => b.r.right - a.r.right)                  
      104 -      .slice(0, 6)                                            
      105 -      .map(                                                   
      103 +      // Leaf-ish: no element child also overflows (so we repo
          +rt the source).                                               
      104 +      .filter(                                                
      105          (x) =>
      107 -          `${x.el.tagName.toLowerCase()}.${(x.el.className || 
          -"").toString().slice(0, 36)} -> ${Math.round(x.r.right)}`,    
      108 -      );                                                      
      106 +          !Array.from(x.el.children).some(                    
      107 +            (c) => c.getBoundingClientRect().right > cw + 1,  
      108 +          ),                                                  
      109 +      )                                                       
      110 +      .sort((a, b) => b.r.width - a.r.width)                  
      111 +      .slice(0, 6)                                            
      112 +      .map((x) => {                                           
      113 +        const ws = getComputedStyle(x.el).whiteSpace;         
      114 +        const txt = (x.el.textContent || "").trim().slice(0, 4
          +0);                                                           
      115 +        return `${x.el.tagName.toLowerCase()}.${(x.el.classNam
          +e || "").toString().slice(0, 30)} w=${Math.round(x.r.width)} w
          +s=${ws} "${txt}"`;                                            
      116 +      });                                                     
      117      return { scrollW: de.scrollWidth, clientW: cw, widest };
      118    });
      119    expect(

‚Źļ Bash(npx playwright test --config=e2e/playwright.config.ts mobile-verify 2>&1
      | grep -E "offenders" | head -10; echo "---- widest fixture lines ----";
      awk '{ print l‚Ä¶)
  ‚éŅ ¬†    Error: horizontal overflow; offenders: ["h2._sectionTitle_6w61b_82
      w=403 ws=normal \"Your GitHub permissions. Your secrets, s\"","p. w=4
     03 ws=normal \"Read the full privacy policy ‚Üí\"","span._comment_6w61b_
     ‚Ä¶ +12 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 3m)

‚Źļ Now the root causes are clear:

  - Trace viewer: no real wide content ‚ÄĒ the offenders (turn-sep,
  thread-controls) are just empty/short elements stretched to 760px. The
  .viewer-body flex item has the default min-width:auto, so it refuses to shrink
  below .thread's max-width:760 and overflows the 391px viewport. Fix:
  min-width:0 on .viewer-body.
  - Landing: the .redact secret-preview <pre> has long unbreakable tokens
  (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-‚Ä¶) under white-space:pre-wrap (which doesn't break
  long words), inflating the single-column privacy grid to 423px. Fix:
  overflow-wrap:anywhere so those tokens wrap.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/styles/viewer.css)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 3 lines
      344      display: block;
      345      padding: 0;
      346      max-width: none;
      347 +    /* As a flex item of .vibeshub-viewer it would otherwise r
          +efuse to shrink                                               
      348 +       below .thread's max-width (760px) and overflow narrow v
          +iewports. */                                                  
      349 +    min-width: 0;                                             
      350    }
      351    .vibeshub-viewer .promptrail {
      352      display: none;

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/routes/Landing.module.css)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 3 lines
      1445  .redact pre {
      1446    margin: 0;
      1447    white-space: pre-wrap;
      1448 +  /* Break long secret tokens (e.g. sk-ant-‚Ä¶) so they don't b
           +low out the                                                  
      1449 +     single-column privacy grid on mobile. */                
      1450 +  overflow-wrap: anywhere;                                   
      1451    font-family: inherit;
      1452    font-size: inherit;
      1453    line-height: inherit;

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 13 lines
      141    await expect(
      142      page.getByRole("heading", { name: "Add startup credential 
           smoke-check" }),
      143    ).toBeVisible();
      144 +  const chain = await page.evaluate(() => {                   
      145 +    let el: HTMLElement | null = document.querySelector(".thre
          +ad-controls");                                                
      146 +    const out: string[] = [];                                 
      147 +    while (el && el !== document.body) {                      
      148 +      const cs = getComputedStyle(el);                        
      149 +      out.push(                                               
      150 +        `${el.tagName.toLowerCase()}.${(el.className || "").to
          +String().slice(0, 24)} ow=${el.offsetWidth} disp=${cs.display}
          + minW=${cs.minWidth} maxW=${cs.maxWidth} ox=${cs.overflowX}`, 
      151 +      );                                                      
      152 +      el = el.parentElement;                                  
      153 +    }                                                         
      154 +    return out;                                               
      155 +  });                                                         
      156 +  console.log("CHAIN:\n" + chain.join("\n"));                 
      157    await page.screenshot({ path: "/tmp/vh-trace.png", fullPage:
            true });

      159  });

‚Źļ Bash(npx playwright test --config=e2e/playwright.config.ts mobile-verify -g
      "trace viewer" 2>&1 | grep -A12 "CHAIN:"; echo "exit: $?")
  ‚éŅ ¬†CHAIN:
     div.thread-controls ow=760 disp=flex minW=0px maxW=760px ox=visible
     div.viewer-main ow=760 disp=block minW=0px maxW=none ox=visible
     ‚Ä¶ +15 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 2m)

‚Źļ .vibeshub-viewer is correctly 390px but its child .viewer-body is 760px ‚ÄĒ and
  there's a _container_ (CSS-module) flex wrapper I haven't seen. Let me inspect
  TraceView's wrapper.

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ Found both mechanisms:

  1. Trace viewer: .vibeshub-viewer is a column flex with default
  align-items:stretch. When .viewer-body's max-content hits .thread's
  max-width:760, the flex line cross-size inflates to 760 and stretches every
  section to 760, overflowing the 390px viewport. Fix: cap the direct children
  to max-width:100% so the line can't inflate.
  2. Landing: .redactPath (~/.claude/projects/‚Ä¶jsonl) is nowrap +
  overflow:hidden but, as a flex child with min-width:auto, its min-content is
  the full path (~280px), inflating the privacy grid column. Fix: min-width:0 so
  the ellipsis actually engages.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/styles/viewer.css)
  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 8 lines
      351    .vibeshub-viewer .promptrail {
      352      display: none;
      353    }
      354 +  /* .vibeshub-viewer is a column flex with align-items: stret
          +ch. Once a section                                            
      355 +     (the thread, capped at --content-width 760px) reports a 7
          +60px max-content,                                             
      356 +     the flex line cross-size inflates to 760 and stretches ev
          +ery section to it,                                            
      357 +     overflowing the viewport. Cap direct children so the line
          + can't inflate. */                                            
      358 +  .vibeshub-viewer > * {                                      
      359 +    min-width: 0;                                             
      360 +    max-width: 100%;                                          
      361 +  }                                                           
      362  }
      363  
      364  /* ---------- hero ---------- */

      1443    white-space: nowrap;

           +h, which inflates                                            
      1445 +     the single-column privacy grid past the viewport on mobi
           +le. */                                                       
      1446 +  min-width: 0;                                              
      1447  }
      1448  .redact pre {
      1449    margin: 0;

‚Źļ Identical numbers ‚ÄĒ so neither last fix took effect. For the trace viewer, my
  .vibeshub-viewer > * rule (specificity 0,1,0) lost to the existing
  .vibeshub-viewer .viewer-body { max-width: none } (0,2,0). The correct fix is
  to cap the elements that actually carry max-width: var(--content-width) (the
  thread/controls/footer) to 100% on mobile ‚ÄĒ that removes the 760px max-content
  that inflates the flex line. For Landing, my redactPath guess was wrong; let
  me also upgrade the diagnostic to pinpoint true overflow sources (scrollWidth 
  > clientWidth).

      | grep -E "sources|passed|failed|‚úď|‚úė" | head -20; echo "exit: $?")
  ‚éŅ ¬†  ‚úė  1 e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts:136:1 ‚Äļ landing has no horizontal ove
     rflow on mobile (3.3s)

     ‚Ä¶ +25 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 3m)

‚Źļ Now I understand both deeply:

  - Trace: max-width:100% is a percentage ‚ÄĒ it's ignored during intrinsic
  (max-content) sizing, so the flex line still inflates to¬†760. I need a
  definite cross-size. Setting width:100% on the direct children (which resolves
  against the definite 390px .vibeshub-viewer) pins each child to 390 and stops
  the line inflation. The wide code blocks already have their own
  overflow-x:auto to scroll internally.
  - Landing: real source is the .privacy section (421px); my redact guesses
  haven't moved it. Let me apply the trace fix and instrument the privacy grid
  to find the true driver.

‚Źļ Update(webapp/frontend/src/styles/viewer.css)

      156 +      e.style.width = "1px";

          +ng().slice(0, 18)} ow=${e.offsetWidth} minc=${minContent(e)}`)

          +s HTMLElement) }))

‚Źļ Both privacy grid items have tiny min-content (139, 90) ‚ÄĒ so privacy can
  shrink and is not the real driver. The installCard/shareCard in sources have
  overflow:hidden (they clip, don't push the page). My sources detector wrongly
  includes self-clipping elements. Let me require the element's own overflow-x
  to be visible to find the genuine driver.

          +) => {

      171 -      out.push(`ITEM ${e.tagName}.${(e.className || "").toStri
          -ng().slice(0, 18)} ow=${e.offsetWidth} minc=${minContent(e)}`)
          -;                                                             
      172 -      // Find the descendant with the largest min-content.    
      173 -      Array.from(e.querySelectorAll("*"))                     
      174 -        .map((d) => ({ d: d as HTMLElement, mc: minContent(d a
          -s HTMLElement) }))

      176 -        .slice(0, 2)                                          
      177 -        .forEach((x) =>

          -toString().slice(0, 18)} minc=${x.mc} ws=${getComputedStyle(x.
          -d).whiteSpace} "${(x.d.textContent || "").trim().slice(0, 24)}
          -"`),                                                          
      179 -        );                                                    
      163 +      // Leaf-ish only, to find true unbreakable sources.

      166 +      if (r.width <= cw - 30) return;

          +, 20)} w=${Math.round(r.width)} ws=${cs.whiteSpace} owr=${cs.o

      170 +      );

      173    });

‚Źļ Bash(npx playwright test --config=e2e/playwright.config.ts mobile-verify -g
      "DEBUG min-content" 2>&1 | grep -A30 "SCAN:" | head -35; echo "exit: $?")
  ‚éŅ ¬†SCAN:
     P._heroSub_1g3d9_228 w=369 ws=normal owr=normal "Your Claude Code sess
     ions, inc"
     ‚Ä¶ +16 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 2m)

‚Źļ The leaf scan shows no genuine unbreakable driver on Landing (the wide
  commentLine spans are inside the install code block's overflow-x:auto
  scroller; the rest just fill their box at ws:normal). Yet .privacyGrid reports
  a 421px scroll width with 390px client width. Let me check the grid's
  computed column template directly ‚ÄĒ I suspect the single-column media rule
  isn't taking.

‚Źļ Bash(cat > /tmp/probe.spec.ts <<'EOF'
      import { test } from "@playwright/test";‚Ä¶)
  ‚éŅ ¬†PROBE {
       "gtc": "401.391px",
       "w": 390,
     ‚Ä¶ +11 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ‚éŅ ¬†(timeout 2m)
‚Źļ The smoking gun: .privacyGrid computes grid-template-columns: 401.391px ‚ÄĒ the
  single 1fr track is sizing to 401px inside a 350px content box. This is the
  classic 1fr min-content blowout (1fr = minmax(auto,1fr), and the auto floor
  refuses to shrink). The fix is minmax(0, 1fr). Let me clean up the probe,
  apply minmax(0,1fr) to the collapsing Landing grids, and fix the trace
  viewer's outcome panel the same way plus min-width:0 on its flex columns.

  Searched for 1 pattern (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ Confirmed: it's the classic 1fr = minmax(auto,1fr) blowout. Let me remove the
  probe and convert the collapsing grids to minmax(0,1fr), fix the trace
  viewer's outcome panel the same way, and let long inline code/PR links wrap.

‚Źļ Bash(rm -f e2e/probe.spec.ts; echo "removed probe")
  ‚éŅ ¬†removed probe

  ‚éŅ ¬†Added 3 lines, removed 1 line
      1925     ==========================================================
            ====== */
      1926  @media (max-width: 980px) {
      1927    .heroGrid {
      1928 -    grid-template-columns: 1fr;                              
      1928 +    /* minmax(0,‚Ä¶) instead of 1fr so a wide child (code block
           +, long line)                                                 
      1929 +       can't push the track past the viewport. */            
      1930 +    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr);

      1932    }

‚Źļ Build passes. One unit test failed ‚ÄĒ AuthWidget.test.tsx queries the sign-in
  link by name. My split into Sign in<span> with GitHub</span> shouldn't change
  the accessible name, so let me check what it actually asserts.
  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ The accessible-name computation across the split text node + span drops the
  inter-word space, breaking the query. The clean fix is an explicit aria-label
  on the link ‚ÄĒ that keeps the full "Sign in with GitHub" name for screen
  readers (even when the visible text is just "Sign in" on mobile) and satisfies
  the test.

‚Źļ Bash(git --no-pager diff --stat src; echo "=== full diff ==="; git --no-pager
      diff src)
  ‚éŅ ¬† webapp/frontend/src/components/AuthWidget.tsx      |  3 +-
      .../frontend/src/components/trace/ViewerTopbar.tsx |  9 ++-
      webapp/frontend/src/routes/Landing.module.css      | 58 +++++++++++++
     ‚Ä¶ +359 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

‚Źļ The diff is clean and fully scoped to mobile breakpoints (plus the safe global
  overflow-x guard and the backward-compatible auth markup). Everything
  verifies.

  Summary

  I fixed the mobile horizontal-scroll and crowding across all three views shown
  in your screenshots. The root causes were more than just "too much stuff" ‚ÄĒ
  there were three distinct layout bugs:

  1. Topbars overflowed the viewport (caused the sideways scroll on the repo +
  trace pages)
  - The header rows are flex with nowrap children (breadcrumbs, the "Sign in
  with GitHub" button) and 32px side padding that didn't fit on a phone.
  - Fixes: 16px padding on mobile; the CTA shows just "Sign in" (full label kept
  for screen readers via aria-label); the trace viewer drops the redundant
  owner crumb (vibeshub / vibeshub / vibeshub ‚Üí vibeshub / repo / trace/‚Ä¶),
  ellipsizes the trace id, and collapses Share to an icon. On the landing page
  the "git for your vibes" tagline (which was wrapping one word per line) is
  hidden on phones.
  - Added a global html { overflow-x: hidden } safety net.

  2. The trace viewer was pinned to 760px ‚ÄĒ .vibeshub-viewer is a column-flex
  with align-items: stretch; the thread's max-width: 760px plus wide content
  (long prompt lines, code) inflated the flex line and stretched every section
  to 760px. Fixed by pinning direct children to a definite width:100% (wide code
  still scrolls inside its own box) and reflowing the outcome panel.

  3. Crowded grids wrapping one word per line ‚ÄĒ the classic 1fr (=
  minmax(auto,1fr)) min-content blowout: browse/trace card titles, the privacy
  preview (long sk-ant-‚Ä¶ tokens), the file-path lists. Fixed with minmax(0,1fr),
  overflow-wrap, min-width:0, and stacking the date below the card body
  (dropping the redundant short-id hash on mobile, as you suggested).

  Verification (all green):
  - A new Playwright spec asserts zero horizontal overflow at 390px on landing,
  repo, and trace pages ‚ÄĒ all pass. Screenshots confirm clean, uncrowded headers
  and readable cards.
  - Unit tests: 168/168 pass; production build passes.
  - The 3 failing e2e tests (auth flow, two viewer tool-card tests) fail 
  identically on clean main ‚ÄĒ they're pre-existing and environment-related (no
  backend on :8000), not from my changes.

  Two notes:
  - I left a regression test at webapp/frontend/e2e/mobile-verify.spec.ts
  (untracked). Keep it as a guard or delete it ‚ÄĒ your call.
  - I made no commit, per your workflow. Want me to commit these on a branch /
  open a PR?

‚úĽ Baked for 39m 50s

‚ÄĽ recap: Goal: fix mobile layout (horizontal scroll, crowded headers/grids) on vibeshub. Done and verified: overflow gone at 390px, tests and build pass. Next: commit the changes on a branch or open a PR if you want. (disable recaps in /config)

‚ĚĮ yes

  PR.
